2606.07309.title: Acoustic Cue Alignment in Audio Language Models for Speech Emotion Recognition
2606.07309.paper_summary: Instruction-following audio language models (ALMs) can be augmented with explicit acoustic cues, yet it remains unclear whether such cues are used in a grounded way when the raw audio is already available. We study this question in speech emotion recognition (SER) by deriving six interpretable acoustic concept tokens from the standardised eGeMAPS paralinguistic feature set. These tokens summarise energy, pitch, dynamics, brightness, formants, and voice quality, and are appended to the textual prompt while the audio input is kept unchanged. Across the widely used FAU-Aibo and IEMOCAP benchmarks, aligned tokens improve unweighted average recall (UAR), whereas shuffled, conflicting, or corrupted tokens reduce performance relative to aligned tokens and shift confusions toward neutral. Importantly, predictions do not collapse under strong token perturbations, suggesting that the models are sensitive to the symbolic cue channel but remain partly anchored to the audio signal. We argue that token-only interventions provide a practical way to probe audio-grounded cue use, robustness, and interpretability in ALM-based affective computing.
2606.07309.paper_summary_zh: 指令遵循的音頻語言模型（ALMs）可以通過明確的聲學線索進行增強，但當原始音頻已經可用時，這些線索是否以有根據的方式使用仍不清楚。我們通過從標準化的eGeMAPS語言學特徵集推導出六個可解釋的聲學概念標記來研究這個問題，應用於語音情感識別（SER）。這些標記總結了能量、音高、動態、亮度、共振峰和音質，並在文本提示中附加，而音頻輸入保持不變。在廣泛使用的FAU-Aibo和IEMOCAP基準測試中，對齊的標記提高了無權重平均召回率（UAR），而隨機、衝突或損壞的標記則相對於對齊的標記降低了性能，並將混淆轉向中性。重要的是，預測在強標記擾動下並未崩潰，這表明模型對符號線索通道敏感，但仍部分依賴於音頻信號。我們認為僅使用標記的干預提供了一種實用的方法來探測基於音頻的線索使用、穩健性和在ALM基礎的情感計算中的可解釋性。
2606.07309.author: Iosif Tsangko et.al.
2606.07309.authors: Iosif Tsangko, Andreas Triantafyllopoulos, Björn W. Schuller
